title: Microchloa tenella
intro: Microchloa tenella, also known as Khas or Bahera, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Microchloa tenella include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Microchloa tenella is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, which are used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal ailments, as well as to promote wound healing and skin health.
taxonomy: Microchloa tenella is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified as a member of the tribe Chlorideae, and within the genus Microchloa. The genus Microchloa is also part of the subfamily Chloridoideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies within the family Poaceae. Microchloa tenella is native to tropical Africa and is known for its small size and delicate appearance. The classification of Microchloa tenella reflects its evolutionary relationships within the family Poaceae.
